page: Amazon Web Services mac-m4max.metal is a bare metal macOS instance powered by Apple silicon. It features 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs using the arm64_mac architecture, running at a clock speed of 4.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is fully dedicated, with one thread per core.

The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this bare metal instance offers high resource density and dedicated hardware for Apple-specific development pipelines. The lack of local storage requires external storage attachment, which represents a structural tradeoff for disk-heavy operations.

This server type is designed for macOS and iOS application development, continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipelines, application building, and testing workloads requiring Apple silicon architecture.
description: Amazon Web Services mac-m4max.metal is a bare metal macOS instance designed for Apple-specific development workloads. It features an Apple arm64_mac CPU with 16 dedicated physical cores operating at 4.0 GHz, providing one thread per core. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or a dedicated GPU, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps. Qualitatively, the instance offers high resource density for specialized macOS environments, though the absence of local storage requires external storage solutions. This hardware profile is optimized for macOS and iOS application building, testing, and CI/CD pipelines.
